title: Scope D T-D5: demote agents_meta_stale from error → warning in doctor
description: In createMetaCheck (doctor.ts:2232-2240), change `agents_meta_stale` severity from `error` to `warning`. Update resolution message: 'Benign — engine auto-heals on next plan-context/get-sections call. Run `fab doctor --fix` for explicit reconciliation.' Existing 'fixable_error' classification → 'fixable_warning' (or whichever severity tier the doctor framework supports for warnings with auto-fix).

files.change: Change `issueCheck(..., 'error', 'fixable_error', 'agents_meta_stale', ...)` to `issueCheck(..., 'warning', 'fixable_warning', 'agents_meta_stale', ...)`. Update message + resolution text per task description (engine auto-heals; --fix for explicit). NOTE: verify the doctor framework supports a 'fixable_warning' tier — if not, use the closest equivalent (likely just 'warning' with the fix-path still wired via the existing doctor.ts:1031-1071 block — that block fixes regardless of severity tier as long as the code is in its list).

Update assertions: (1) stale meta produces a warning (not error) check entry; (2) `fab doctor` exits with code 0 (warning doesn't fail); (3) `fab doctor --fix` still reconciles the stale meta (via existing reconcile block at 1031); (4) resolution text references auto-heal.

implementation: Inspect doctor.ts framework for warning severity tier — confirm 'fixable_warning' or equivalent exists
Update createMetaCheck stale branch (lines 2232-2240) to use warning severity
Verify the fixable-codes registry at doctor.ts:1031-1071 still includes 'agents_meta_stale' (it should — the demotion is about exit-code severity, not fix-path participation)
Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/server test doctor` to confirm green
Manually verify: `fab doctor` with stale meta exits 0 (not 1)

reference.pattern: Existing warning-severity checks in doctor.ts (use grep for `'warning'`)
reference.examples: Locate a peer warning check (e.g., some lint rules) and mirror its severity/type pairing

criteria: agents_meta_stale renders as warning, not error
`fab doctor` with stale meta exits code 0
`fab doctor --fix` still reconciles agents_meta_stale (existing fix path unbroken)
Resolution message references 'engine auto-heals on next plan-context/get-sections call'

rationale.chosen_approach: Demote severity tier; preserve fix path
alternatives_considered: Remove the check entirely (loses operator visibility into drift)
Keep as error (CI false-positives once auto-heal lands)
decision_factors: Operator still wants drift surfaced for transient cases
rationale.tradeoffs: Slight risk that operators ignore the warning permanently (mitigated by hook banner from T-D4 surfacing actual heal events)

manual_checks: Run `fab doctor` on a synthesized stale-meta repo, confirm exit code 0 + warning text
